prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `6a11f6da`.
codex_response:
Implemented `6a11f6da` as a new ARC2 task package. The rule is: split the `15x5` input into three stacked `5x5` layers and overlay them with priority `6 > 1 > 8 > 0`. The summary hint matched the official examples exactly, so I used it; no `notes.md`, `helpers.py`, or `dsl_ext.py` change was needed.

The package is in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_6a11f6da/__init__.py), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_6a11f6da/verifier.py#L4), and [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_6a11f6da/generator.py#L38). The generator explicitly samples three color-specific `5x5` masks with densities close to the official family, constructs the overlaid output directly, and only keeps examples whose visible `0/1/8/6` counts stay in-family. No added primitive was necessary.

Validation passed:
- `python -m arc2.shared.verify --task 6a11f6da`: `6/6` official examples passed
- `python -m arc2.shared.build --task 6a11f6da --n-examples 1000`: built [task.json](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/6a11f6da/task.json), [originals.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/6a11f6da/previews/originals.png), [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/6a11f6da/previews/preview_01.png), [preview_02.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/6a11f6da/previews/preview_02.png), and [preview_03.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/6a11f6da/previews/preview_03.png)
- `./arc2_verify_generated_all.sh --task 6a11f6da`: `1000/1000` generated examples passed

I also sanity-checked representative generated samples and the first preview sheet; the outputs still read as the same stacked-layer overlay family rather than arbitrary random noise.
